mission: Help internal product and technology teams build, ship, and operate software faster and more safely by treating the platform as an internal product portfolio with strong golden paths, self-service workflows, and measurable engineering outcomes.

businessModel: Internal productivity leverage across product engineering teams
Reduced duplicated foundation work across the technology organization
Lower operational risk through stronger default controls
Faster modernization by moving teams onto supported paths
Improved engineering capacity allocation through platform reuse
marketForces: Growing software-delivery complexity across cloud, security, and compliance domains
Pressure to improve developer experience without weakening governance
Need to reduce cognitive load on stream-aligned teams
Higher expectations for software supply-chain integrity and production resilience
Expansion of specialist engineering needs such as data and ML platforms

deliveryPrinciples: Treat internal developers and tech leaders as customers with clear product expectations.
Prefer paved roads over bespoke consultation, but keep explicit escape hatches for valid specialist needs.
Measure platform success through adoption, flow, reliability, and reduced cognitive load rather than tool count.
Encode security and compliance into workflows so the standard path is also the safest path.
Run platform engineering as a portfolio of internal products with explicit ownership and lifecycle management.

deliveryFlows.name: Team onboarding flow
deliveryFlows.description: A team discovers the supported path, scaffolds a service, provisions environments, activates telemetry, and ships through reusable pipelines.

deliveryFlows.name: Shared runtime evolution flow
deliveryFlows.description: Platform teams improve runtime, delivery, identity, and observability layers while preserving product-team contracts and migration clarity.

deliveryFlows.name: Incident and platform recovery flow
deliveryFlows.description: Shared observability, service ownership, and automation guide cross-team response when the platform or a major dependency fails.

deliveryFlows.name: Governance and exception flow
deliveryFlows.description: Required controls run automatically in templates and pipelines, while exceptions are explicit, time-bound, and evidence-backed.

organizationSizing.assumedCompanyContext: A technology organization with roughly 60-90 software teams across product, platform, data, and specialist engineering domains.

groups.mission: Own the portal, templates, developer workflows, and support loops that make the platform discoverable and adoptable.
Provide the runtime, environment, delivery, and integration foundations that most product teams should use by default.
Embed identity, supply-chain, telemetry, and incident automation into the normal delivery path.
Measure platform impact, guide migrations, and keep customer research and platform adoption grounded in evidence.
teams: Platform enablement and field engineering team

operatingModelNotes: The platform should stay thin enough to reduce cognitive load rather than becoming an additional layer of mandatory complexity.
Platform teams need real product management, customer research, and adoption metrics, not only technical ownership.
Enablement work should be time-bound and designed to move demand back onto reusable self-service paths.
Specialist platforms such as data and ML should share foundations where leverage is high, but keep explicit product boundaries.
